title: Plan capacity for a vector index
description: Estimate index memory from vector count, dimensionality, and index overhead before provisioning.
body: Use when sizing hardware for a vector workload. Actions: compute raw vector bytes as rows times dimensions times 4 (float32), add the index structural overhead (HNSW graph edges scale with the connectivity parameter; IVFFlat adds list centroids), and include headroom for query-time work and growth. Compare the total to available RAM and decide between scaling up, quantizing, or sharding. Verify by building on a representative subset and extrapolating linearly. Validation signals include the measured index size matching the estimate and the build fitting within the memory budget. Risk boundary: underestimating HNSW overhead causes a build-time OOM that can take down the instance, so validate on a subset and leave explicit headroom before a full build.
